denial.reason_text: Member has completed only 2 weeks of physical therapy. Standard pathway requires minimum 6 weeks. Pharmacotherapy requirement not met: only one agent trialed. Member does not meet conservative treatment requirements under CC-SPINE-2024 Section 2.2.
denial.reviewer: Dr. Alan Foster, MD — HealthFirst Utilization Management

appeal_basis: Member has severe myelomalacia on MRI — California law prohibits PT requirement when structural neurological compromise is present
Treating physician declares PT contraindicated due to documented cord signal change
Regulatory override under CIC 10169.5(a)(3) makes plan criteria irrelevant

clinical_record.presenting_diagnosis: Progressive cervical myelopathy C5-C6 with myelomalacia

initial_presentation.notes: Patient presents with 4-month history of bilateral hand clumsiness and gait instability. Upper motor neuron signs present at initial visit.

mri_1.findings: Severe central cord compression C5-C6. T2 hyperintensity within cord (myelomalacia). No prior imaging for comparison but cord signal change indicates existing injury.

neurological_exam.findings: Bilateral Hoffman signs, hyperreflexia bilateral upper and lower extremities, positive Babinski bilateral, gait ataxia documented

physical_therapy.reason_for_discontinuation: Treating physician discontinued PT after 2 weeks — documented that PT was causing symptom exacerbation. Written order to discontinue in chart.
physical_therapy.outcome_note: Symptoms worsened during PT. Physician declared PT contraindicated.

physical_therapy_summary.note: PT discontinued after 2 weeks per physician order. Myelomalacia present at time PT was initiated. PT contraindicated per treating physician.

agents.response: Temporary mild improvement, no sustained benefit

clinical_record.physician_declaration: Dr. Kim letter dated 2024-10-15: PT is contraindicated in this patient. Myelomalacia is present on initial MRI. Continuing PT risks irreversible cord injury. CIC 10169.5(a)(3) exception applies. Surgery is urgently indicated.

question: Should the prior authorization denial for ACDF C5-C6 (CPT 22551) be overturned on appeal?

ground_truth_complexity.obvious_reading: UPHOLD — member completed only 2 weeks PT, only one pharmacotherapy agent, no interventional procedure. Plan criteria clearly not met.
ground_truth_complexity.harder_question_1: Does it matter that the plan criteria are not met if CIC 10169.5(a)(3) prohibits requiring PT at all?
ground_truth_complexity.harder_question_2: The physician says PT was contraindicated from the start because myelomalacia was present on the initial MRI — does this mean the PT requirement was never legally applicable?
ground_truth_complexity.harder_question_3: Can a plan deny based on criteria that California law prohibits applying?
ground_truth_complexity.harder_question_4: If the regulatory override applies, does the answer change based on whether the member tried PT at all?
ground_truth_complexity.the_genuine_tension: Plan criteria clearly not met — 2 weeks PT, one drug, no injection. A single-pass reviewer reads this as UPHOLD. But myelomalacia was present on the initial MRI, which triggers CIC 10169.5(a)(3): the PT requirement cannot be applied when structural neurological compromise is present. The plan's criteria are legally unenforceable here regardless of how much PT was done.
ground_truth_complexity.right_answer: OVERTURN — The regulatory override under CIC 10169.5(a)(3) applies from the moment myelomalacia appeared on the initial MRI. The plan cannot require PT completion when objective structural neurological compromise is documented. Plan criteria are legally unenforceable. A system that reaches UPHOLD based on plan criteria alone has the wrong answer and missed the controlling legal provision.
